title: 10 best herbal teas for dry cough
intro_desc: Herbal teas for dry cough are a natural remedy that provides relief from irritating and persistent coughs. They work by soothing the throat and lungs, reducing inflammation, and loosening mucus, which helps to ease congestion and discomfort. Examples of herbal teas that help with dry cough include peppermint, ginger, and slippery elm. Drinking these teas can improve lives by providing instant relief from dry cough symptoms, allowing individuals to sleep better, eat more comfortably, and engage in daily activities without the constant need for cough drops or medication.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with dry cough because its natural ingredients work to soothe and calm the throat, providing instant relief from irritations. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and discomfort in the airways, while its expectorant properties loosen mucus, allowing it to be easily cleared out. As a result, herbal licorice teas provide a gentle and effective way to alleviate dry cough symptoms, promoting a restful night's sleep and improved overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin and glycyrrhetic acid, which help reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs, soothing a dry and irritated cough.

Expectorant properties: The saponins present in licorice root have expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the airways, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion.

Soother of mucous membranes: Licorice tea's demulcent properties help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat and lungs, reducing irritation and inflammation, and providing relief from a dry and scratchy cough.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of licorice plant contain compounds like glycyrrhizin and liquiritin, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, making them effective in soothing a dry cough.
Barks: The barks of licorice plant are used to make teas that can help relieve chest congestion and coughing fits by reducing inflammation and relaxing the respiratory tract.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried licorice root powder or two teaspoons of fresh licorice root per eight ounces of boiling water.
Bring the mixture to a boil then reduce heat and simmer for five to ten minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth to remove the herb particles.
Add honey to taste if desired and stir well to combine.
Drink the tea hot immediately after preparation while it is still warm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme teas helps with dry cough because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the throat and respiratory tract. The essential oils present in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to reduce inflammation and congestion, providing relief from dry cough symptoms. Additionally, thyme's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, allowing for easier breathing and a more productive cough. Overall, herbal thyme teas offer a natural and effective remedy for dry cough sufferers.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial Properties: Thyme's antibacterial compounds, such as thymol, help combat the underlying bacterial infections that cause dry coughs, reducing their severity and duration.

Expectorant Properties: Thyme's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, relieving congestion and making it easier to cough up phlegm, providing relief from dryness and irritation.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can exacerbate dry cough symptoms, and alleviate discomfort and pain associated with coughing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots of Thymus species have been traditionally used to soothe respiratory issues and calm dry cough.
Seeds: Containing essential oils and flavonoids, thyme seeds are known to reduce inflammation and alleviate congestion associated with dry cough.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality thyme leaves from a trusted source or purchase organic dried thyme flowers.
Combine one teaspoon of dried thyme flowers with one cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbal properties to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large cup, discarding the solids.
Drink the warm tea immediately and repeat every 2-3 hours as needed, ideally at bedtime for best results.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint teas helps with dry cough because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the irritated throat lining. The menthol in peppermint also helps to thin mucus, making it easier to expel, and relieves congestion. Additionally, the tea's calming effects can help reduce inflammation and irritation caused by a dry cough, providing fast and effective relief from discomfort and annoyance.
remedy_properties: Menthol content: Menthol, a key component of peppermint oil, helps to thin mucus and relieve congestion, making it easier to expel phlegm and alleviate dry cough symptoms.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds present in peppermint tea help reduce inflammation in the airways, which can exacerbate dry coughs, while also soothing irritated mucous membranes.
Expectorant properties: Peppermint tea's expectorant properties help loosen and clear out phlegm from the lungs and bronchi, providing relief from stubborn dry coughs and congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Peppermint roots are valued for their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to calm a dry, irritated throat.
Leaves: Fresh or dried peppermint leaves are commonly used in tea blends due to their natural decongestant and expectorant properties, making it an effective remedy for relieving dry cough.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves or purchase dried peppermint in sufficient quantity.
Measure one teaspoon of dried peppermint or two tablespoons of fresh peppermint for each cup.
Heat filtered water in a teapot or kettle to boiling point for five minutes preparation time.
Steep the measured peppermint in hot water for seven to ten minutes steeping time.
Strain and serve the tea immediately or store in an airtight container in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower teas helps with dry cough because its active compound, triterpenoid saponins, exhibits anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the mucous membranes in the throat and chest. The tea's flavonoids also provide antioxidant protection, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that can exacerbate dry cough symptoms. Additionally, the tea's natural expectorant properties help loosen and clear out mucus, relieving congestion and allowing for easier breathing. As a result, Kansas coneflower teas offer a natural, effective way to alleviate dry cough discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: The flavonoids and triterpenes present in Kansas Coneflower tea have been shown to exhibit antiviral properties, which can help combat viral infections that cause dry cough.
Anti-inflammatory effects: The saponins and phenolic acids in the tea have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the throat and chest, alleviating symptoms of dry cough.

Mucilage production: Kansas Coneflower contains a type of soluble fiber called mucilage, which can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by dry cough.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots have anti-inflammatory and antitussive properties, making them effective in relieving dry cough symptoms.
Barks: With its rich flavonoid content, barks help soothe irritated respiratory tissues and reduce inflammation, providing relief from dry cough.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one ounce of dried echinacea angustifolia root or one teaspoon of loose-leaf herbal mixture containing echinacea angustifolia for each cup of water.
Boil filtered water in a pot and let it cool down for 5-7 minutes before pouring it over the echinacea angustifolia herb.
Steep the herbs in boiling water for 5-10 minutes to release the bioactive compounds responsible for soothing dry coughs.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids, then pour the liquid into a cup containing honey if desired.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature before drinking it slowly and repeatedly throughout the day as needed to alleviate symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena teas helps with dry cough because of its natural expectorant properties, which loosen and clear mucus from the airways. The tea's soothing and calming effects also help to reduce inflammation in the throat, providing instant relief from irritation and discomfort. Additionally, lemon verbena's antioxidant-rich compounds help to boost the immune system, making it easier for the body to fight off underlying infections that may be exacerbating the cough. As a result, herbal lemon verbena teas provide a natural and effective way to alleviate dry cough symptoms without harsh chemicals or additives.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal lemon verbena tea helps to reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, which can alleviate symptoms of a dry cough by decreasing swelling and irritation.

Antiviral and antimicrobial properties: Lemon verbena's antiviral and antimicrobial properties help to combat viral and bacterial infections that can cause dry coughs, reducing the severity and duration of the infection.

Expectorant and mucolytic properties: Herbal lemon verbena tea's expectorant and mucolytic properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to expel and relieve congestion and coughing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the lemon verbena plant contain expectorant compounds that help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, providing relief for dry cough.
Leaves: The leaves of the lemon verbena plant have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe irritated throat tissues and reduce coughing.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried verbena officinalis flowers or two teaspoons of fresh leaves.
Combine the measured verbena officinalis with one cup of boiling water in a medium-sized pot.
Reduce heat to low and let steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbal mixture to infuse.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth, discarding solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking, enjoying the soothing relief from dry cough.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Amla teas helps with dry cough because of its unique combination of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants. The tea's high concentration of vitamin C and flavonoids helps to soothe and calm the irritated mucous membranes in the throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with dry coughs. Additionally, amla's natural expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus, providing relief from persistent hacking and coughing fits. Overall, drinking amla tea can provide quick and effective relief from dry cough symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Amla tea's high antioxidant content helps to reduce inflammation and protect the mucous membranes in the throat, relieving discomfort associated with dry cough.
Expectorant properties: The tea's natural expectorants (such as vitamin C and flavonoids) help loosen and clear out mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing coughing fits.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Amla tea's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat and lungs, which can exacerbate dry cough symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Amla roots are good for making medicinal teas for dry cough as they contain a rich source of mucilage, which helps to soothe and calm irritated throat tissues.

Fruits: Amla fruits are commonly used to make teas for dry cough due to their high concentration of vitamins C and E, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the airways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one teaspoon of dried phyllanthus emblica powder or fresh fruit from the indian gooseberry tree.
Boil one cup of filtered water in a pot for five minutes to create a hot beverage base.
Add one teaspoon of phyllanthus emblica powder or two tablespoons of chopped fresh fruit to the boiling water.
Steep the mixture for seven to nine minutes, allowing the herbs to infuse and release their medicinal properties.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, discarding the solids and serving hot with honey or lemon to taste.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion teas helps with dry cough because it is rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals that soothe and calm irritated throats. The tea's natural anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammation and swelling in the throat, allowing for easier mucus production and relief from congestion. Additionally, dandelion's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out excess mucus, making it easier to expel and relieving symptoms of dry cough. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like taraxasterol, which can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the throat, providing relief from dry cough.

Expectorant properties: The mucilage present in dandelion tea acts as a natural expectorant, loosening mucus and phlegm in the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion caused by dry cough.

Antioxidant activity: Dandelion tea's antioxidant properties can help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate dry cough and other respiratory issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps soothe and calm irritated respiratory tissues, relieving dry cough symptoms.
Flowers: Containing flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and congestion associated with dry cough.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 dried taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in a clean container for the tea blend.
Measure out one teaspoon of the dried herb blend per cup of boiling water for optimal dosage.
Steep the herbal mixture in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes to allow for maximum extraction of beneficial compounds.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean teapot to remove any loose particles.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before serving and drinking as needed throughout the day for soothing relief from dry coughs.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary teas helps with dry cough because of its expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways. The herb's natural oils also soothe and calm the throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with dry coughs. Additionally, rosemary's antioxidant-rich compounds help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to the development of chronic coughs. By drinking rosemary tea, individuals can experience relief from dry cough symptoms and improved respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Antitussive properties: Rosemary tea helps to soothe and calm a dry cough, reducing inflammation and relieving discomfort, making it easier to breathe and sleep.
Expectorant properties: The expectorant compounds in rosemary tea help to loosen and clear out mucus from the respiratory tract, reducing congestion and providing relief from stubborn coughs.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Rosemary's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation in the airways, alleviating dryness and irritation that can contribute to a persistent cough.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots are a rich source of essential oils, which help soothe and calm dry coughs by reducing inflammation in the throat.
Leaves: Rosemary leaves have a natural expectorant property that helps loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, providing relief from dry cough.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves in amounts of one to two teaspoons.
Rinse the rosmarinus officinalis leaves with warm water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine the rinsed rosmarinus officinalis leaves with 250 milliliters of boiling water for a steeping time of five minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large cup or teapot to discard solids.
Allow the tea to cool for one to two minutes before drinking and consuming immediately after preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mallow teas helps with dry cough because it contains mucilages, which are thick, protective substances that soothe and calm irritated throats. When consumed as a tea, mallow's mucilages coat the throat and chest, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by dry air or allergens. This helps to loosen and remove excess phlegm, providing relief from dry, hacking coughs. Additionally, mallow's anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the throat, allowing for easier breathing and a more restful night's sleep.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Mallow tea contains a high amount of mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and protects the mucous membranes in the throat, helping to alleviate dryness and discomfort associated with dry cough.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in mallow tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat and lungs, making it easier to breathe and alleviating coughing fits.

Demulcent activity: Mallow tea's demulcent properties create a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, trapping moisture and reducing inflammation, which helps to soothe and calm dry coughs, allowing for more effective healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the mallow plant are rich in mucilage, a thick, soothing substance that helps to calm and protect the respiratory tract.
Flowers: The flowers of the mallow plant contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and ease coughing symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh malva sylvestris flowers or purchase dried flowers from a reputable source in sufficient quantities for the desired amount of tea.
Combine one teaspoon of dried malva sylvestris flowers or two teaspoons of fresh flowers with one cup of boiling water.
Allow the mixture to steep for five to seven minutes to release the active compounds and flavor.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a teapot or cup to remove the solids.
Drink the tea hot or allow it to cool to room temperature, consuming two to three cups per day as needed for relief from dry cough.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder teas helps with dry cough because of its natural expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear out mucus from the airways. The soothing compounds present in elder flowers, such as flavonoids and terpenes, also calm and reduce inflammation in the throat, providing relief from irritation and discomfort caused by dry coughs. Additionally, elder tea's antimicrobial properties may help combat underlying infections that can exacerbate dry coughs, making it a natural and effective remedy for soothing and alleviating dry cough symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial compounds: Elder tea contains antimicrobial compounds like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which help combat the underlying infection causing the dry cough by inhibiting the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi.
Expectorant properties: The tea's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, relieving congestion and reducing the severity of dry cough symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Elder tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and anthocyanins, which can help reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, alleviating discomfort and pain associated with dry cough.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elder roots contain saponins, which help soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the throat, reducing the severity of dry cough symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Elder rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, making them effective in alleviating dry coughs.
Barks: Elder barks contain phenolic compounds that exhibit expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, providing relief from dry cough.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried elderberry flowers of sambucus nigra for optimal potency and flavor.
Measure one tablespoon of dried elderberry flowers per eight ounces of boiling water in a heat-resistant teapot.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes, allowing the active compounds to infuse and release their therapeutic properties.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container, discarding the solids and preserving the liquid extract.
Enjoy the warm, soothing tea as needed, ideally two to three times daily, for a period of seven to ten days for maximum relief from dry cough symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger teas helps with dry cough because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the irritated throat. The warm liquid helps to loosen and clear out mucus, reducing congestion and discomfort. Additionally, ginger's expectorant properties help to thin and expel phlegm, making it easier to breathe and relieving the persistent coughing spasms. This natural remedy provides instant relief, calming the throat and chest while promoting overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce inflammation in the throat and bronchial passages, which can alleviate the discomfort and dryness associated with a dry cough.

Expectorant properties: The volatile oils present in ginger, such as citral and limonene, have been shown to increase mucus production and help loosen congestion, making it easier to expel phlegm from the lungs and relieving a dry, hacking cough.

Antitussive properties: Ginger's active compounds, including gingerol and paradol, possess antitussive properties that can help soothe an irritated throat and reduce the urge to cough, providing relief from a persistent dry cough.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, rhyzomes help relieve congestion and soothe a dry cough.
Roots: Containing natural expectorants, roots help loosen mucus and reduce coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger roots of zingiber officinale of good quality.
Wash the harvested ginger roots thoroughly with clean water to remove dirt and impurities.
Dry sliced or chopped ginger roots in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours to preserve flavor.
Steep one teaspoon of dried ginger powder or two slices of fresh ginger in one cup boiling distilled water for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and add honey if desired then drink immediately while still warm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with dry cough because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The camphor and linalool present in lavender help to relax the airways, reducing congestion and inflammation that can exacerbate a dry cough. Additionally, the calming effects of lavender can help to ease tension and anxiety related to coughing, making it easier to rest and recover. As a natural expectorant, English lavender tea also helps to loosen and clear mucus from the chest, providing relief from stubborn dry coughs.
remedy_properties: Antitussive property: Lavender's antitussive property helps to relieve and calm a dry, irritated cough by reducing the urge to cough, promoting relaxation, and soothing the throat.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lavender tea can help reduce inflammation in the airways, which can contribute to chronic coughing, and alleviate congestion and discomfort in the chest and throat.

Antioxidant and antibacterial properties: Lavender's antioxidants and antibacterial properties help to combat infection-causing bacteria and viruses that may be contributing to the dry cough, while also protecting the respiratory tract from further damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenoids, which help soothe and calm dry coughs by reducing inflammation and spasms in the respiratory tract.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and terpenes that exhibit anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, making them effective in relieving dry cough symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one teaspoon of dried lavender flowers or four teaspoons of fresh lavender leaves and stems.
Combine the harvested lavender with one cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant teapot or mug.
Steep the mixture for five to seven minutes, allowing the essential oils to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the lavender solids and discard.
Allow the tea to cool before drinking, sweetening with honey if desired, and enjoying within 30 minutes of preparation.

Combine one teaspoon of the herb material with one cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for seven to ten minutes to allow the active compounds to release.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean cup.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenrod teas helps with dry cough because it contains compounds that help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, providing instant relief from congestion. Barks: The barks are used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and irritation in the throat, providing relief from the scratchiness associated with dry cough.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried solidago virgaurea leaves and flowers in sufficient quantity for one cup of tea.
Combine one teaspoon of the herb with one cup of boiling water and let it steep for five minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a teapot or mug, discarding the solids.
Add honey to taste if desired, as it is known to soothe a dry cough.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking and enjoy its benefits throughout the day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel teas helps with dry cough because of its natural expectorant properties, which loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the lungs and airways. The tea's anethole compound has been shown to relax the smooth muscles in the bronchial tubes, reducing inflammation and easing congestion. Additionally, fennel's anti-inflammatory effects help soothe the irritated mucous membranes, providing fast relief from dry coughing fits.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers from your garden or local health food store weighing approximately 20 grams.
Rinse the harvested herbs gently with cold running water removing any dirt or debris then pat them dry with a clean cloth.
Combine the rinsed herbs with boiling water in a large saucepan pouring 250 milliliters of water for every 10 grams of herbs used.
Allow the mixture to steep for at least 5 minutes and up to 10 minutes depending on your desired strength of tea.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a teapot or cup discarding the solids and serving immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea teas helps with dry cough because it provides a soothing warmth that relaxes the throat and reduces irritation. The natural anti-inflammatory properties in herbs such as ginger, peppermint, and thyme help to reduce swelling and ease congestion, making it easier to expectorate mucus. Additionally, herbal tea's humidifying effects help to loosen phlegm and bring relief from dry coughing fits, promoting a good night's sleep and overall respiratory health.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbs like ginger, turmeric, and cinnamon contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the airways, which can contribute to a dry, hacking cough.
Expectorant properties: Herbs like thyme, eucalyptus, and peppermint have expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, relieving congestion and coughing.

Buds: The buds contain flavonoids and phenolic compounds that help to relax the bronchial muscles, relieving coughing fits and promoting mucus clearance.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried green tea leaves or one green tea bag.
Heat filtered water in a teapot to a boil then let it cool for two minutes.
Steep the green tea leaves in the hot water for three to five minutes.
Add one half teaspoon of dried honey and stir well until fully dissolved.
Strain the tea into a cup and serve immediately while still warm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop teas helps with dry cough because of its natural expectorant properties, which loosen and clear out excess mucus from the lungs and airways. The tea's active compounds, such as flavonoids and saponins, have a soothing effect on the throat and bronchial tubes, reducing inflammation and irritation that can exacerbate coughing. Additionally, maypop tea's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce the production of excess mucus, providing long-lasting relief from dry, hacking coughs.
remedy_properties: Alkaloids: Maypop tea contains alkaloids such as pasleythrine, which have expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, relieving congestion and coughing.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in maypop tea, particularly quercetin, have anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs, making it easier to breathe and reducing coughing.
Saponins: Saponins in maypop tea have antitussive properties, meaning they can help suppress or relieve coughing by decreasing the sensitivity of the respiratory tract and calming the cough reflex.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps soothe and calm irritated throats, making it an effective treatment for dry coughs.
Rhyzomes: Containing compounds like glycosides and flavonoids, rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce throat congestion and relieve dry cough symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh passiflora incarnata leaves and flowers in the early morning or evening when they are most fragrant.
Dry the harvested material by tying it in small bunches and hanging upside down in a warm well-ventilated area for 1 week.
Grind dried passiflora incarnata into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder to create a consistent texture.
Steep 2 teaspoons of the ground passiflora incarnata in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes before straining and serving hot or warm.
Consume the tea 3 times daily as needed, allowing at least 2 hours between servings to maximize benefits for dry cough relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hyssop teas helps with dry cough because of its expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear out mucus from the respiratory tract. The tea's natural anti-inflammatory compounds also reduce swelling in the throat and bronchial tubes, making it easier to expel mucus. Additionally, hyssop contains compounds that have antispasmodic effects, helping to relax the muscles in the airways and calm dry, hacking coughs. By soothing and clearing the respiratory tract, hyssop teas provide natural relief from dry cough symptoms.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Hyssop tea helps loosen and expel mucus from the lungs, relieving congestion and coughing. This allows for easier clearing of the airways and reduces discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Hyssop's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling in the respiratory tract, which can contribute to dryness and irritation that exacerbate coughs. By reducing inflammation, hyssop tea helps soothe the throat and lungs.

Antiviral and antibacterial properties: Hyssop's antiviral and antibacterial components help combat underlying infections that may be causing or contributing to a dry cough, such as the common cold or bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Hyssop roots are valued for their ability to soothe and calm the respiratory tract, making them effective in treating dry coughs.
Leaves: The leaves of the hyssop plant contain expectorant properties, which help loosen mucus and reduce congestion associated with dry coughs.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried hyssopus officinalis leaves and flowers in a small bowl.
Measure out one teaspoon of the herb per cup of boiling water using digital scale.
Steep the herb mixture for seven to ten minutes in boiling water with timer set.
Strain the liquid through fine-mesh sieve into large heat-resistant measuring cup.
Allow the tea to cool and then store it in refrigerator for up to three days.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat dry cough with herbal teas is to brew soothing infusions from ingredients like licorice root, slippery elm, and marshmallow root. These herbs provide natural expectoration and mucilages that help to calm and lubricate the throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Drink 2-3 cups of tea per day, sweetened with honey if desired, for optimal relief from dry cough symptoms.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dry cough that are treated with herbal teas are bronchitis, asthma, and allergies. The soothing properties of herbs like slippery elm, licorice root, and marshmallow root help to calm irritated airways and reduce inflammation. Teas made from these herbs can also ease sinus pressure and congestion, providing relief from symptoms similar to a dry cough.
